synopsis: The Standing Judicial Commission sustained the complaint of the Session of First Presbyterian Church, Augusta, finding that Savannah River Presbytery erred in annulling the Session's actions to dissolve the official relationship with eleven ruling elders. The SJC held that BCO 25-2 does not prohibit a Session from adding agenda items to congregational meetings called pursuant to petitions, and that BCO 24-6 does not prevent a Session from informing the congregation of this right and recommending its exercise.
